diagrams.title: Classification of Multiple Access Protocols
diagrams.description: A hierarchical tree diagram showing the three main categories of MAC protocols: Random Access, Controlled Access, and Channelization. This helps students understand the overall organization of MAC protocols and where specific protocols fit.
elements: Branch 1: Random Access (ALOHA, CSMA, CSMA/CD, CSMA/CA)
Branch 2: Controlled Access (Reservation, Polling, Token Passing)
diagrams.purpose: To provide an overview of all MAC protocol categories and their relationships, establishing the mental framework for the entire topic

diagrams.title: Pure ALOHA and Slotted ALOHA Comparison
diagrams.description: A timeline visualization showing frames being transmitted over a shared channel. Two parallel timelines compare Pure ALOHA (continuous time) with Slotted ALOHA (synchronized slots). Collisions are highlighted in red, successful transmissions in green.
diagrams.purpose: To visually demonstrate the collision problem in random access protocols and explain why Slotted ALOHA improves efficiency by synchronizing transmission times

diagrams.title: CSMA/CD Protocol Flowchart
diagrams.description: A flowchart showing the decision process in Carrier Sense Multiple Access with Collision Detection. Starting from 'Ready to transmit', it shows the carrier sense, transmission, collision detection, and backoff algorithm steps.
elements: Action rectangle: Wait random backoff time
diagrams.purpose: To explain the step-by-step operation of CSMA/CD, helping students understand how stations sense the channel, detect collisions, and recover using the exponential backoff algorithm
